mixLCA 1.0.1

    • Concomitant predictors and distal outcomes

  • Reference

Introduction to mixLCA

Ali Sanaei

2026-05-28

Source: vignettes/mixLCA.Rmd
mixLCA.Rmd

What mixLCA does

mixLCA fits finite mixture models for latent class analysis with a partitioned architecture: concomitant predictors enter through a multinomial logistic regression on class membership; manifest indicators (continuous, categorical, or mixed) define the measurement model; distal outcomes are estimated after the measurement model converges, under Bollen-Croon-Hagenaars inverse-classification-error weighting. The partition prevents distal outcomes from leaking into class meaning and concomitants from contaminating the measurement structure.

The package supports two responses to local dependence among manifest indicators:

  • Direct effects for categorical items (Hagenaars-Vermunt specification search via auto_bvr()).
  • Spectral Local Dependence (SLD) for categorical items: a low-rank decomposition of the conditional Burt matrix (auto_sld()).

For continuous indicators, the covariance structure can be fully unrestricted, diagonal, or sparsified via graphical lasso (dependence = "penalized").

A minimal example 

library(mixLCA)
data("PimaIndiansDiabetes2", package = "mlbench")
pima <- na.omit(PimaIndiansDiabetes2)

fit <- fit_lca(pima,
               continuous  = c("glucose","pressure","mass","pedigree"),
               concomitant = ~ age,
               n_classes   = 2,
               control     = lca_control(n_starts = 10, seed = 110))

summary(fit, data = pima)
plot(fit, type = "profiles")

distal(fit, pima, diabetes ~ age, family = "binomial")

Further reading

The package ships three companion vignettes:

  • vignette("workflow") walks through a categorical LCA from scratch on the poLCA::election data: naive fit, BVR diagnostics, auto_bvr() specification search, and SLD comparison. Read this first if your data are categorical.

  • vignette("distal-covariates") covers concomitant predictors (character vector vs. formula), the predict() contract with na.action-style padding, and BCH distal estimation on the Pima diabetes data.

  • vignette("sld-theory") explains the mathematics of Spectral Local Dependence: the conditional Burt matrix, the rank-dd projector, identification, and the M-step. Read this if you want to understand what SLD is doing and when it is the right tool.

Function index

Core fitting:

  • fit_lca(), lca_control(): entry point and optimiser settings.
  • auto_bvr(): BVR-guided specification search.
  • auto_sld(): adaptive Spectral Local Dependence rank selection.
  • enumerate_lca(): fit a range of KK values.

Diagnostics:

  • fit_indices(): AIC, BIC, aBIC, entropy, ICL.
  • bvr_tests(), bvr_categorical(): bivariate residual diagnostics.
  • class_table(): modal-class x posterior cross-tabulation.

Inference:

  • predict() (S3 method): posteriors, modal class, full diagnostic frame.
  • distal(): BCH-weighted regression for distal outcomes.
  • concomitant_se(), continuous_se(): asymptotic standard errors.
  • get_posteriors(), get_loadings(), coef(): accessors.

Visualisation:

  • plot() (S3 method) with type = c("profiles", "bvr", "distal", "uncertainty", "convergence", "categorical", "spectral_scree", "spectral_loadings").

Session info 

sessionInfo()
